description: Contamination check results for Tenacious-Bench v0.1 partitions

checks.description: 8-gram overlap between train and held_out partitions (candidate_output.email_body)
checks.finding: All programmatic tasks share template phrases in candidate_output.email_body by design. The injectors (inject_fc3_overclaim, inject_fc2_seg4_bypass, etc.) reuse fixed-phrase templates across partitions. This is expected and intentional — the benchmark tests whether the rubric detects these patterns, not whether the exact wording is unique. Input-level contamination (enrichment_brief) is checked by CC-004.
checks.disposition: ACCEPTED — template overlap in candidate outputs is by design; enrichment inputs are unique (CC-004)

checks.description: Cosine similarity of email_body embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) between train and held_out
checks.note: Requires sentence-transformers — scheduled for Day 4 (Colab environment)

checks.description: 8-gram overlap between dev and held_out partitions (candidate_output.email_body)
checks.finding: Same as CC-001 — template phrase overlap in candidate outputs by design. Input-level check (CC-004) shows 0 collisions.
checks.disposition: ACCEPTED — template overlap in candidate outputs is by design

checks.description: SHA-256 hash of enrichment_brief to detect duplicate inputs across partitions
checks.finding: 0 collisions between held_out and train; 0 collisions between held_out and dev. All enrichment_brief combinations are unique across partition boundaries.

checks.description: Verify held_out tasks authored after train/dev tasks to prevent future leakage
checks.result: All partitions authored on 2026-04-27. Held-out was sealed immediately after generation on the same date. Same-day generation and sealing is acceptable — the held-out partition was not used for any downstream decision prior to sealing.

summary.overall_status: clean (pending CC-002 embedding check for Day 4)
summary.notes: Primary contamination risk (input leakage) confirmed clean via CC-004 (0 enrichment_brief hash collisions). Template phrase overlap in candidate outputs (CC-001, CC-003) is by design and does not constitute evaluation contamination. CC-002 embedding similarity check deferred to Colab Day 4.
